response.justification: While this candidate demonstrates strong senior-level engineering skills and diverse technical breadth, they lack critical hard requirements for this embedded security networking role. Most significantly, native-level Japanese proficiency is not evident anywhere in the resume. More fundamentally, their entire career has focused on web development, full-stack engineering, and high-level systems rather than embedded firmware, security technologies, or networking protocols. There is no evidence of C programming expertise for UNIX/embedded systems, security domain knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption systems), or networking fundamentals (Layer 2/3, switch operation, NETCONF, SNMP). The skill gap spans the technical core of the position.
strengths: Senior-level engineer with 10+ years demonstrating ability to own complex systems through full development lifecycle
Demonstrated software craftsmanship through TDD, CI/CD, and infrastructure practices that support quality embedded development
Fluent English communication and some Linux exposure through infrastructure and open-source platform work
concerns: No evidence of native-level Japanese language proficiency, which is explicitly required
Career focused entirely on web, full-stack, and high-level languages (TypeScript, Node.js, D, Java) with minimal embedded systems or C programming experience
Complete absence of security domain expertise—no mention of encryption, authentication systems, MACsec, 802.1X, or attack detection technologies

key_factors.factor: Embedded systems and C/UNIX programming experience
Security and networking embedded firmware background
